enrichment.short_description: Vimeo's virtual event platform for hosting livestreams, webinars, and online conferences with analytics and CRM integration.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vimeo Events lets teams host live webinars and virtual conferences with multi-camera production, chat, polling, and real-time analytics.

features.title: Live streaming & webinars
features.description: Host live or pre-recorded events with multi-camera switching and graphics overlays.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: Add logos and lower-thirds to events to match company branding.

features.title: Interactive engagement
features.description: Audience polling, live chat, and moderated Q&A during events.

features.title: Simulive broadcasting
features.description: Schedule pre-recorded content to stream as if it were live.

features.title: Automatic recording
features.description: Streams are automatically saved for on-demand viewing afterward.

features.title: Real-time analytics
features.description: Track attendance, engagement, and viewer behavior during and after events.

features.title: CRM integration
features.description: Connect with HubSpot and Salesforce to capture leads from event registrations.

features.title: Multi-platform streaming
features.description: Broadcast simultaneously to social platforms and custom RTMP destinations.

pros: Built on Vimeo's established video infrastructure
CRM integrations (HubSpot, Salesforce) support lead capture from events
Simulive lets teams pre-record events while presenting them as live
Real-time analytics track attendee engagement
cons: Advanced plan pricing starts at a business-level price point, not aimed at hobbyists
Enterprise features like SSO require contacting sales for custom pricing
Full feature list and lower-tier pricing are not published in detail on the marketing page

faqs.question: What is Vimeo Events used for?
faqs.answer: It is used to host livestreams, webinars, and virtual conferences with production tools, audience engagement features, and analytics.

faqs.question: Does Vimeo Events support pre-recorded content as a live event?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Simulive feature lets you schedule pre-recorded content to broadcast as if it were streaming live.

faqs.question: Can Vimeo Events integrate with a CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with HubSpot and Salesforce to help capture and route event leads.

faqs.question: What does the Advanced plan include?
faqs.answer: The Advanced plan is $75/month billed annually and includes 10 seats, 7TB of storage, webinar hosting, and analytics.

faqs.question: Is there an Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Vimeo offers a custom Enterprise plan with more seats, storage, dedicated support, and SSO authentication.

use_cases.title: Marketing webinars
use_cases.description: Run branded webinars with lead capture routed directly into a CRM.

use_cases.title: Virtual conferences
use_cases.description: Produce multi-session virtual conferences with interactive audience features.

use_cases.title: Internal communications
use_cases.description: Broadcast company-wide town halls or training sessions with analytics on attendance.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vimeo Events?
paragraphs: Vimeo Events is Vimeo's virtual event platform for hosting livestreams, webinars, and online conferences. It provides multi-camera switching, graphics overlays, and custom branding for professional-looking broadcasts.
Beyond streaming, the platform includes interactive tools like polling, live chat, and moderated Q&A, plus real-time analytics and CRM integrations with HubSpot and Salesforce for lead capture.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Vimeo Events is for
paragraphs: Vimeo Events is built for marketing and communications teams, educators, and enterprise organizations that need to run webinars or virtual conferences with production quality and audience engagement built in.

enrichment.short_description: Browser-based live streaming and recording studio for multistreaming to platforms like YouTube and Facebook.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: StreamYard is a browser-based live streaming studio that lets creators multistream, record, and produce broadcasts without extra equipment.

features.title: Browser-based studio
features.description: Stream and record directly from a browser with no extra software installation.

features.title: Multistreaming
features.description: Broadcast to multiple destinations at once, including custom RTMP destinations on paid plans.

features.title: On-screen participants
features.description: Bring guests into a broadcast, with participant limits varying by plan.

features.title: Cloud and local recording
features.description: Record streams locally or to the cloud depending on the plan.

features.title: Custom overlays and branding
features.description: Add logos, backgrounds, and custom layouts to broadcasts, removing the StreamYard watermark on paid plans.

features.title: AI clips and backgrounds
features.description: AI-assisted clip generation and virtual backgrounds available on the Core plan and above.

features.title: On-Air webinars
features.description: Host webinars directly through the platform on the Advanced plan.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required to start
Runs entirely in the browser with no downloads for hosts or guests
Multistreams to several destinations simultaneously on paid plans
Used by a large base of creators (StreamYard states 12,000,000+ creators)
cons: Free plan adds a StreamYard watermark and limits local recording to 2 hours/month
Multistreaming to more than one destination requires a paid plan
Business-level plans require contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: Is StreamYard free?
faqs.answer: Yes, StreamYard has a free plan with essential studio features, though it includes a StreamYard logo, streaming limits, and only 2 hours of local recording per month.

faqs.question: What platforms can StreamYard stream to?
faqs.answer: It supports streaming to platforms like Facebook and YouTube, with multistreaming to more destinations on paid plans, plus custom RTMP destinations.

faqs.question: Does StreamYard offer a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, both the Core and Advanced plans include a 7-day free trial.

faqs.question: What's the difference between the Core and Advanced plans?
faqs.answer: Advanced adds 4K local recordings, multistreaming to 8 destinations, more backstage participants, On-Air webinars, custom fonts, and transcript downloads.

faqs.question: Does StreamYard require guests to download software?
faqs.answer: No, StreamYard runs in the browser, so guests can join broadcasts without installing anything.

use_cases.title: Live podcast recording
use_cases.description: Record and stream podcast episodes with guests joining from a browser.

use_cases.title: Multi-platform broadcasting
use_cases.description: Simultaneously stream a broadcast to several social platforms from one session.

use_cases.title: Webinars
use_cases.description: Host On-Air webinars directly within StreamYard on the Advanced plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is StreamYard?
paragraphs: StreamYard is a professional live streaming and recording studio that runs entirely in the browser. It lets creators produce broadcasts and record content without specialized equipment or software installation.

The platform supports multistreaming to platforms like YouTube and Facebook, custom branding with logos and overlays, and features like AI clips, cloud recording, and On-Air webinars on higher-tier plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who StreamYard is for
paragraphs: StreamYard is built for content creators, podcasters, and businesses who want to livestream or record polished video content without a dedicated studio setup, and it states it is trusted by over 12 million creators.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered platform for recording, editing, and publishing studio-quality podcasts, video, and webinars remotely.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Riverside is an AI-powered recording and editing platform for podcasts, video interviews, live streams, and webinars.

features.title: Local 4K recording
features.description: Records each participant's video and audio locally at up to 4K/48kHz for high-quality, sync-safe files.

features.title: AI-powered editing
features.description: Text-based editor with filler-word removal, speech correction, and auto-layout generation.

features.title: AI Co-Creator
features.description: Generates clips, thumbnails, and headlines from recorded sessions for promotion.

features.title: AI translation and dubbing
features.description: Translates and dubs content into 30+ languages.

features.title: Live streaming
features.description: Streams simultaneously to platforms like YouTube, LinkedIn, Instagram, and Twitch.

features.title: Podcast hosting
features.description: Hosts and publishes podcasts with automatic distribution to major listening apps.

features.title: Webinar hosting
features.description: Runs webinars with registration, Q&A, chat, and analytics on the Webinar plan.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required
Local recording per participant avoids sync and dropout issues common with browser-based capture
Extensive AI toolset including translation/dubbing into 30+ languages
Publishes directly to major podcast and video platforms
14-day free trial available on paid plans
cons: Free plan limited to 2 hours of multi-track recording (one-time) and includes a watermark
Higher-tier features like webinars and unlimited studios require the more expensive Webinar or Business plans
Business plan pricing is custom/quote-only

faqs.question: Is Riverside free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Riverside offers a free plan with 2 hours of one-time multi-track recording, up to 720p video, and a Riverside watermark.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan include?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan ($29/month, or $24/month billed annually) includes 1 studio, up to 4K video and 48kHz audio, no watermark, unlimited text-based editing, AI editing, and podcast hosting.

faqs.question: Can Riverside host webinars?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Webinar plan ($99/month, or $79/month billed annually) supports webinars with up to 100 registrants, custom branded registration forms, and analytics.

faqs.question: Does Riverside support live streaming?
faqs.answer: Yes, from the Grow plan upward it supports Full HD live streaming and multistreaming to unlimited destinations.

faqs.question: What platforms does Riverside run on?
faqs.answer: Riverside is web browser-based and also offers a Mac app and mobile apps for iOS and Android.

use_cases.title: Remote podcast recording
use_cases.description: Record separate high-quality local tracks with remote guests and edit via a text-based editor.

use_cases.title: Video content repurposing
use_cases.description: Use AI Co-Creator to turn long recordings into social clips, thumbnails, and headlines.

use_cases.title: Multilingual content distribution
use_cases.description: Translate and dub recordings into 30+ languages for international audiences.

use_cases.title: Webinar hosting
use_cases.description: Run branded webinars with registration, Q&A, and lead capture.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Riverside?
paragraphs: Riverside is an AI-powered platform for recording, editing, repurposing, and distributing video and audio content. It records each participant locally at up to 4K video and 48kHz audio, avoiding the sync issues common with browser-based remote recording.

The platform layers on AI tools for text-based editing, filler-word removal, clip and thumbnail generation, and translation/dubbing into 30+ languages, plus podcast hosting, live streaming, and webinar hosting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Riverside is for
paragraphs: Riverside targets podcasters, video creators, marketers, and businesses running webinars or interviews who want studio-quality remote recording plus AI-assisted editing and repurposing in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one platform for recording, AI editing, hosting, and distributing podcasts and video content.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Zencastr is an all-in-one podcasting platform offering remote recording, AI editing and clipping, hosting, and analytics.

features.title: Remote recording
features.description: Records 4K multi-track video and 48kHz WAV audio on desktop and mobile.

features.title: AI editing (ZenAI)
features.description: Text-based editing for audio and video content powered by ZenAI.

features.title: AI clipping
features.description: Automatically generates social media clips from longer recordings.

features.title: Transcription
features.description: Produces transcriptions the company states are 99% accurate.

features.title: Hosting and analytics
features.description: Hosts audio and video content with built-in listener/viewer analytics.

features.title: Monetization tools
features.description: Includes built-in earning options for creators.

features.title: Team collaboration
features.description: Supports multi-user workspaces for teams producing shows together.

features.title: Embeddable player & Video RSS
features.description: Distribute episodes via an embeddable player or Video RSS feed.

pros: All-in-one workflow covering recording, editing, hosting, and distribution
AI-powered editing and automatic clip generation via ZenAI
Free tier available to get started
Supports both audio and 4K video recording
cons: Detailed pricing tiers and exact costs are not published in an easily accessible format on the site
AI features are tied to the ZenAI system, so capabilities depend on plan tier

faqs.question: What is Zencastr used for?
faqs.answer: Zencastr is used to record, edit, host, and distribute podcasts and video content from a single platform.

faqs.question: Does Zencastr have AI editing?
faqs.answer: Yes, its ZenAI system powers text-based editing and automated social clip generation.

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Zencastr?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zencastr offers a free tier to get started, alongside paid plans including a Hobbyist tier.

faqs.question: Can Zencastr record video?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports 4K multi-track video recording in addition to 48kHz WAV audio.

faqs.question: Does Zencastr provide transcripts?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers transcription the company describes as 99% accurate.

use_cases.title: Remote podcast recording
use_cases.description: Record separate high-quality audio and video tracks with remote guests.

use_cases.title: Social clip creation
use_cases.description: Use AI clipping to turn long-form episodes into short social media clips.

use_cases.title: Podcast hosting and distribution
use_cases.description: Host episodes and distribute via embeddable player and Video RSS.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Zencastr?
paragraphs: Zencastr is an all-in-one platform for creating, editing, and distributing audio and video content. It combines remote recording of 4K video and 48kHz WAV audio with AI-powered editing and clipping through its ZenAI system.

Beyond recording and editing, Zencastr provides hosting, transcription, an embeddable player, Video RSS distribution, and monetization tools, aiming to reduce the number of separate tools creators need to juggle.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Zencastr is for
paragraphs: Zencastr is built for podcasters, video creators, and interviewers of all skill levels who want recording, AI-assisted editing, hosting, and distribution in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based remote recording studio with progressive uploads, multi-track editing, and AI-assisted post-production.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SquadCast is a cloud recording studio for podcasters with progressive uploads, separate audio/video tracks, and AI tools.

features.title: Progressive Uploads
features.description: Proprietary technology auto-saves recordings during the session to protect against connectivity loss.

features.title: Separate audio/video recording
features.description: Captures each participant's audio and video separately to avoid drift and sync issues.

features.title: Multi-track editing
features.description: Adjust individual tracks for cleanup, processing, and volume correction.

features.title: Cloud backup
features.description: Keeps multiple backup copies of recordings to protect against data loss.

features.title: In-browser interface
features.description: Guests join sessions in the browser with no downloads or installation required.

features.title: AI suite
features.description: Includes AI features such as transcription and AI speech tools, with usage limits scaling by plan.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with tools including Zapier, Dolby, Dropbox, and Descript.

pros: Free plan available to try the platform
Progressive Uploads protect recordings from connectivity failures during a session
Separate audio/video tracks per participant reduce sync issues
Used by organizations such as Google, Spotify, NPR, Microsoft, and ESPN per the company's site
In-browser guest experience requires no software installation
cons: Free plan limited to 1 recording hour per editor per month and exports with watermarks
AI features are capped by monthly usage limits that scale with plan tier
Higher recording-hour and team-size needs require the more expensive Business plan

faqs.question: Is SquadCast free?
faqs.answer: Yes, SquadCast has a free plan offering 1 recording hour per editor per month, 1 show, and exports up to 720p with watermarks.

faqs.question: What does Progressive Uploads do?
faqs.answer: It's SquadCast's proprietary technology that auto-saves recordings during a session, protecting against connectivity loss or technical failures.

faqs.question: Does SquadCast separate audio and video tracks?
faqs.answer: Yes, it records each participant's audio and video separately, which the company says eliminates drift and sync issues common in remote recording.

faqs.question: What's included in the Hobbyist plan?
faqs.answer: The Hobbyist plan ($16/month billed annually or $24/month billed monthly) includes 10 recording hours/editor/month, 5 shows, unlimited watermark-free exports up to 1080p, and a basic AI suite.

faqs.question: Do guests need to install software to join a SquadCast session?
faqs.answer: No, SquadCast runs in the browser, so guests can join without downloads or installation.

use_cases.title: Remote podcast recording
use_cases.description: Record separate high-quality audio/video tracks with remote guests, protected by auto-saving uploads.

use_cases.title: Audiobook and voice-over recording
use_cases.description: Capture studio-quality remote sessions for narration work.

use_cases.title: Video content production
use_cases.description: Record video alongside audio for YouTube or other video platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SquadCast?
paragraphs: SquadCast is a cloud-based recording studio built for remote collaboration on audio and video content. Its Progressive Uploads technology auto-saves recordings during a session to protect against connectivity loss or technical failures.
The platform records each participant's audio and video separately to avoid drift, offers multi-track editing, cloud backups, and an in-browser guest experience with no downloads required.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SquadCast is for
paragraphs: SquadCast serves podcasters, YouTubers, audiobook narrators, voice-over artists, and producers, with the company citing organizations such as Google, Spotify, NPR, Microsoft, and ESPN among its users.

enrichment.short_description: Browser-based software for remote, low-latency, studio-quality audio recording and live interviews.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cleanfeed is browser-based software for low-latency, studio-quality remote audio recording, used by broadcasters and podcasters.

features.title: Studio-quality audio recording
features.description: Records high-definition audio remotely with minimal latency.

features.title: Multi-guest support
features.description: Records multiple guests with isolated audio tracks per person.

features.title: Browser-based access
features.description: Runs in the browser without complex setup for hosts or guests.

features.title: Multitrack recording
features.description: Available on Pro plan for separating and processing individual tracks.

features.title: Privacy controls, markers, and notes
features.description: Studio tools for managing sessions, included on the Pro plan.

features.title: Audio repair and volume controls
features.description: Built-in tools to clean up and balance recorded audio on Pro plan.

features.title: Centre-stage video
features.description: DCI 2K, 60fps video recording available on the Cinema plan.

pros: Free tier (Cleanfeed Lite) available, including for commercial use
Low-latency connections designed for live remote interviews
Won a Primetime Emmy Award for its audio software
Used by major media organizations including the BBC, Global, Cumulus Media, Warner Bros., Discovery, and NBCUniversal
Works entirely in the browser with no complex setup required
cons: Free Lite plan is capped at 2 guests
Video recording (Centre-stage video) requires the significantly more expensive Cinema plan
Enterprise licensing for 5+ seats requires contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: Is Cleanfeed free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cleanfeed Lite is a free plan that supports up to 2 guests and unlimited recording time, including for commercial use.

faqs.question: What's included in Cleanfeed Pro?
faqs.answer: Cleanfeed Pro (from $12/month personal, $36/month business USD) supports up to 32 guests and adds multitrack recording, privacy controls, markers and notes, audio repair, and volume controls.

faqs.question: Does Cleanfeed support video?
faqs.answer: Video recording (Centre-stage video, DCI 2K at 60fps) is available on the Cleanfeed Cinema plan.

faqs.question: Has Cleanfeed won any awards?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cleanfeed won a Primetime Emmy Award for pioneering audio software.

faqs.question: Who uses Cleanfeed?
faqs.answer: Cleanfeed is used by podcasters, radio broadcasters, and major media organizations including the BBC, Global, Cumulus Media, Warner Bros., Discovery, and NBCUniversal.

use_cases.title: Remote radio interviews
use_cases.description: Conduct low-latency, high-definition remote interviews for broadcast.

use_cases.title: Podcast recording
use_cases.description: Record multi-guest podcast episodes with isolated audio tracks per guest.

use_cases.title: Voice-over and film production
use_cases.description: Capture professional remote audio sessions for commercial or film production.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cleanfeed?
paragraphs: Cleanfeed is browser-based software for remote, low-latency, studio-quality audio recording and live interviews. It lets users record with guests anywhere in the world without complex setup.

The Pro plan adds multitrack recording, privacy controls, audio repair, and volume tools, while the Cinema plan adds high-resolution video recording. Cleanfeed has won a Primetime Emmy Award and is used by major broadcasters including the BBC and NBCUniversal.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cleanfeed is for
paragraphs: Cleanfeed is built for podcasters, radio broadcasters, voice actors, film and television productions, commercial audio producers, and news organizations that need reliable, low-latency remote recording.

enrichment.short_description: Remote recording and livestreaming platform with local 4K capture, AI transcript-based editing, and automated post-production.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Boomcaster is a remote recording and livestreaming platform with local 4K capture, AI editing, and automated post-production.

features.title: Local 4K recording
features.description: Captures 4K video at 30fps and uncompressed WAV audio locally on each participant's device to prevent data loss.

features.title: Multi-platform livestreaming
features.description: Streams to YouTube, LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitch, Instagram, X, and custom RTMP endpoints.

features.title: AI Editor
features.description: Transcript-based editing to remove silences and unwanted sections.

features.title: LiveEdit Production Tools
features.description: Real-time layout switching, guest controls, and green screen management during a session.

features.title: Automatic post-production
features.description: Combines tracks, syncs audio, and inserts branding automatically after recording.

features.title: Studio customization
features.description: Branded logos, backgrounds, and personalized guest links.

features.title: Real-time closed captions
features.description: Live captions available in 30+ languages.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required to start
Local per-participant recording protects against data loss from dropped connections
Streams to six major platforms simultaneously plus custom RTMP
AI transcript-based editing speeds up post-production
Yearly billing offers 12 months for the price of 10
cons: Paid plans are metered by recording/livestreaming hours, with per-hour overage charges beyond plan limits
Enterprise features like SSO/SAML and SOC2 compliance require a custom-priced plan
Guests limited to 3 backstage participants across the listed plans

faqs.question: Is Boomcaster free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Boomcaster offers a free plan, described as free forever, with no credit card required to start.

faqs.question: What does the Starter plan include?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $20/month and includes 5 hours of studio-quality recording and livestreaming, with $4.20 charged per additional hour.

faqs.question: Does Boomcaster support livestreaming to multiple platforms?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can livestream to Facebook, YouTube, LinkedIn, Twitch, Instagram, X, and custom RTMP endpoints simultaneously.

faqs.question: Does Boomcaster offer AI editing?
faqs.answer: Yes, its AI Editor provides transcript-based editing to remove silences and unwanted sections, alongside automated transcription.

faqs.question: Is there an Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Boomcaster offers a custom-priced Enterprise plan with SSO/SAML integration, SOC2 compliance, and dedicated support.

use_cases.title: Remote podcast recording and livestreaming
use_cases.description: Record local 4K tracks with guests while simultaneously livestreaming to multiple platforms.

use_cases.title: Branded content production
use_cases.description: Use LiveEdit and studio customization to produce branded marketing videos or webinars.

use_cases.title: Multi-language live events
use_cases.description: Deliver real-time closed captions in 30+ languages during live sessions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Boomcaster?
paragraphs: Boomcaster is a remote recording and livestreaming platform that captures 4K video and uncompressed WAV audio locally on each participant's device, then automatically syncs and combines the tracks in post-production.

It supports simultaneous livestreaming to platforms like YouTube, LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitch, Instagram, and X, alongside an AI Editor for transcript-based editing and LiveEdit tools for real-time production control.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Boomcaster is for
paragraphs: Boomcaster is designed for solo podcasters, agencies, content networks, and businesses producing marketing videos, webinars, or training content that need reliable local recording plus multi-platform livestreaming.

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one podcast maker that automates recording, audio cleanup, editing, hosting, and publishing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Alitu is an all-in-one podcast maker that automates recording, audio cleanup, editing, hosting, and one-click publishing.

features.title: Built-in recorder
features.description: Record audio/video calls directly, or upload recordings from external sources.

features.title: AI-assisted editing
features.description: Transcript-based editing interface that lets users edit episodes at 2x speed.

features.title: Automatic audio cleanup
features.description: Removes background noise, normalizes volume, and enhances voice quality automatically.

features.title: Magic Filters
features.description: Automatically removes filler words and silence from episodes.

features.title: Podcast hosting
features.description: Free hosting up to 1,000 downloads/month, distributing to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and a custom website.

features.title: AI-generated show notes and transcripts
features.description: Generates show notes and transcripts, with transcripts available in 17 languages.

features.title: One-click publishing
features.description: Schedules and publishes episodes across distribution platforms in one click.

pros: Combines recording, editing, hosting, and publishing in a single tool aimed at non-technical users
Automatic noise removal, normalization, and filler-word cutting reduce manual editing work
Free hosting included up to 1,000 downloads/month
7-day free trial with full feature access and a 30-day money-back guarantee
cons: Premium hosting beyond 1,000 downloads/month costs extra ($10/month for up to 10,000 downloads)
Standard plan is a single-show subscription; additional shows cost extra (2 shows priced at $63/month)
Done-for-you editing service is a separate, considerably higher-priced add-on starting at $295/month

faqs.question: What is Alitu used for?
faqs.answer: Alitu is an all-in-one podcast maker that automates recording, audio cleanup, editing, hosting, and publishing so episodes can be produced in about 20 minutes.

faqs.question: How much does Alitu cost?
faqs.answer: The Standard plan is $38/month, or $32/month billed annually, with a 7-day free trial and a 30-day money-back guarantee.

faqs.question: Does Alitu offer podcast hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, free hosting is included for up to 1,000 downloads/month, with premium hosting available for $10/month for up to 10,000 downloads.

faqs.question: Can Alitu produce show notes automatically?
faqs.answer: Yes, it generates AI show notes and provides transcripts in 17 languages.

faqs.question: Does Alitu offer a done-for-you editing service?
faqs.answer: Yes, a premium editing service is available starting at $295/month (first month $1), with weekly or fortnightly plans and a 4-day turnaround.

use_cases.title: Beginner podcast production
use_cases.description: Record, automatically clean up, and publish episodes with minimal audio production experience.

use_cases.title: Fast episode turnaround
use_cases.description: Use Magic Filters and transcript-based editing to produce episodes in around 20 minutes.

use_cases.title: Multi-show podcasting
use_cases.description: Run more than one show under a multi-show pricing plan.
use_cases.audience: Podcast networks and hosts with multiple shows

features: Multi-show pricing, saves $13/month versus individual plans

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Alitu?
paragraphs: Alitu is an all-in-one podcast maker that automates the technical parts of podcast production, including recording, audio cleanup, transcript-based editing, hosting, and one-click publishing to platforms like Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

Its automatic noise removal, volume normalization, and Magic Filters (which strip filler words and silence) are designed to let creators finish an episode in about 20 minutes without prior audio editing experience.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Alitu is for
paragraphs: Alitu is aimed at non-technical creators who want to reduce the editing workload of podcasting and publish consistently, with a 7-day free trial and 30-day money-back guarantee to try it risk-free.

enrichment.short_description: Audio editing software for journalists and podcasters, combining transcript-based and waveform editing.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hindenburg PRO is audio editing software for journalists and podcasters, combining transcript-based and waveform editing.

features.title: Transcript-based editing
features.description: Edit audio by editing its transcript, alongside traditional waveform editing.

features.title: Clipboard System
features.description: Organize and navigate large amounts of raw recorded material.

features.title: Video Track
features.description: Supports audio post-production work for video projects.

features.title: Manuscript
features.description: A word processor-style interface for editing audio.

features.title: Multitrack recording
features.description: Records multitrack sessions for debates or roundtable discussions.

features.title: Transcription
features.description: Built-in transcription supporting 99 languages, with offline processing available; hours per month vary by plan.

features.title: Broadcast-ready loudness
features.description: Publishing tools output audio at broadcast-ready loudness levels.

pros: Purpose-built for spoken-word editing (journalism, podcasts, radio) rather than music production
Combines transcript-based editing with traditional waveform editing in one workspace
Transcription supports 99 languages with an offline processing option
Separate Field Recorder iPhone app and Narrator audiobook product extend the product line
Endorsed publicly by James Cridland, founder of Podnews.net
cons: Exact subscription and perpetual license prices are not published on the main marketing pages
Transcription hours are limited on lower tiers (e.g., Plus is capped at 20 hours/month, Premium at 50 hours/month)
No free tier is indicated; only a trial is available

faqs.question: What is Hindenburg used for?
faqs.answer: Hindenburg PRO is audio editing software designed for spoken-word production such as journalism, podcasting, and radio broadcasting.

faqs.question: Does Hindenburg offer transcript-based editing?
faqs.answer: Yes, it combines a Manuscript-style, word-processor interface for transcript-based editing with traditional waveform editing.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial of Hindenburg?
faqs.answer: Yes, a trial version is available; Hindenburg does not appear to offer a permanent free tier.

faqs.question: What licensing options does Hindenburg offer?
faqs.answer: It offers Standard, Plus, and Premium subscription tiers billed monthly or yearly, as well as a perpetual license option, with separate licensing for Personal, Business, and Education use.

faqs.question: Does Hindenburg have a mobile recording app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Hindenburg Field Recorder is a separate iPhone app for field recording.

use_cases.title: Podcast and radio editing
use_cases.description: Edit spoken-word episodes using transcript-based and waveform editing tools.

use_cases.title: Field recording
use_cases.description: Capture audio in the field using the companion iPhone app.

use_cases.title: Audiobook production
use_cases.description: Produce audiobooks using the Narrator product built on the same editing workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hindenburg?
paragraphs: Hindenburg (Hindenburg PRO) is audio editing software built specifically for spoken-word production rather than music. It combines transcript-based editing with a Manuscript word-processor-style interface and traditional waveform editing.

The product line also includes Narrator for audiobook production and a separate Field Recorder iPhone app, alongside features like multitrack recording, 99-language transcription, and broadcast-ready loudness output for publishing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hindenburg is for
paragraphs: Hindenburg is aimed at independent journalists, podcasters, radio broadcasters, and audio storytellers, along with educational institutions, organizations, and non-profits that produce spoken-word audio content.

enrichment.short_description: Podcast hosting platform with unlimited shows, video podcast support, AI transcription, and dynamic ad insertion.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Transistor.fm is a podcast hosting platform offering unlimited shows, video podcast support, analytics, and AI transcription.

features.title: Unlimited podcast hosting
features.description: Hosts an unlimited number of shows on one account without per-show charges.

features.title: Video podcast support
features.description: Supports video podcasts with HLS distribution.

features.title: Private podcasts
features.description: Restrict episodes to members-only or team audiences.

features.title: Automatic distribution
features.description: Publishes automatically to major podcast apps and players.

features.title: AI transcription
features.description: Generates AI transcripts for episode content, sold as an add-on.

features.title: Podcast website builder
features.description: Automatically generates a podcast website for each show.

features.title: Advanced analytics
features.description: Tracks downloads, subscribers, and listening trends.

features.title: Dynamic ad insertion
features.description: Supports pre-roll, mid-roll, and post-roll dynamic ads, available from the Professional plan.

pros: Unlimited shows and team members on every plan, without per-show fees
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Supports video podcasts with automatic YouTube posting on Professional plan and above
Built-in podcast website and network website included
API access included for developers
cons: Branding removal requires the Business plan ($99/month)
AI transcription is a separate paid add-on rather than included in base plans
Usage beyond 250K monthly downloads or 3K private subscribers requires custom Enterprise pricing

faqs.question: How much does Transistor cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $19/month for the Starter plan (20K monthly downloads), up to $99/month for Business (250K monthly downloads), with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: Does Transistor offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Can I host multiple podcasts on Transistor?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include unlimited podcasts and unlimited team members on one account.

faqs.question: Does Transistor support video podcasts?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports video podcasts with HLS distribution, and Professional plan and above can auto-post episodes to YouTube.

faqs.question: Is transcription included in Transistor plans?
faqs.answer: AI transcription is available as a paid add-on (5 or 20 hours/month) rather than bundled into the base plans.

use_cases.title: Independent podcast hosting
use_cases.description: Host and distribute a podcast to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, and other apps from one dashboard.

use_cases.title: Private/members-only podcasts
use_cases.description: Restrict episode access to paying members or internal teams.

use_cases.title: Podcast networks
use_cases.description: Manage multiple shows under one account with a shared network website.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Transistor?
paragraphs: Transistor (Transistor.fm) is a podcast hosting and distribution platform that lets creators publish audio and video podcasts to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube, and Pocket Casts from a single dashboard.
Every plan includes unlimited shows and team members, with paid tiers scaling by monthly download volume and adding features like dynamic ad insertion, private podcasts, and YouTube auto-posting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Transistor is for
paragraphs: Transistor targets independent podcasters, content creators, brands, and podcast networks that want an all-in-one hosting solution without per-show pricing, backed by a 14-day free trial.
